content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 21,
199’
Sam McCall
Chief Legal Counsel
California Union of Safety Employees
2029
H Street
Sacramento,
CA
95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-95-085
Dear Mr. McCall:
This letter confirms the advice I provided to you on behalf
of the California Union of Safety Employees
(CAUSE)
regarding the
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) 1
In our telephone conversation of March 6,
1995,
you asked
whjther CAUSE would have reporting obligations if CAUSE forgave a
$13,000 loan that CAUSE made to its sponsored committee.
As we
discussed in our telephone conversation, the forgiveness of the
$13,000 loan could be considered an contribution which could
qualify CAUSE as a committee pursuant to Section 82013(c).
However, Regulation 18419(c)
provides that the sponsor will
not qualify as a committee so long as:
(1)
The sponsor does not make or receive a sufficient amount
of contributions or independent expenditures, other than those in
support of its sponsored committee, to satisfy the thresholds set
forth in Government Code Section 82013.
(2)
The sponsored committee reports all contributions and
expenditures made in support of the committee by the sponsor its
intermediate units,
and the members of such entities.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000-18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-95—085
Page
2
(3)
The sponsored committee reports as an intermediary the
sponsor. . . ;
and
(4)
A responsible officer of the sponsor, as well as the
treasurer of the sponsored contmittee,erifies the conuuittee’s
campaign statement pursuant to Government Code Section 81004.
If all of these conditions are met,
CAUSE will not qualify as
a committee by virtue of forgiving the $13,000 loan to its
sponsored committee.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322_590l.2
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
a1 Counsel
2
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10 per page.
Wallace
1,
Legal Division

sections.question: Whether CAUSE would have reporting obligations if CAUSE forgave a $13,000 loan that CAUSE made to its sponsored committee.
sections.conclusion: If all of these conditions are met, CAUSE will not qualify as a committee by virtue of forgiving the $13,000 loan to its sponsored committee: (1) The sponsor does not make or receive a sufficient amount of contributions or independent expenditures, other than those in support of its sponsored committee, to satisfy the thresholds set forth in Government Code Section 82013; (2) The sponsored committee reports all contributions and expenditures made in support of the committee by the sponsor its intermediate units, and the members of such entities; (3) The sponsored committee reports as an intermediary the sponsor; and (4) A responsible officer of the sponsor, as well as the treasurer of the sponsored committee, verifies the committee's campaign statement pursuant to Government Code Section 81004.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether the California Union of Safety Employees (CAUSE) would trigger reporting obligations by forgiving a $13,000 loan to its sponsored committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: CAUSE would not qualify as a committee due to forgiving the loan if four conditions are met: the sponsor does not exceed contribution/expenditure thresholds independent of its sponsored committee, the sponsored committee reports all related contributions and expenditures, the sponsor is reported as an intermediary, and responsible officers verify the campaign statement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an FPPC response letter providing telephone advice confirmation. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers present, but question and conclusion are clearly identifiable in body text. Minor OCR issues noted ('whjther' for 'whether', 'contmittee' for 'committee', 'erifies' for 'verifies') but do not impair understanding.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether CAUSE would have reporting obligations if CAUSE forgave a $13,000 loan that CAUSE made to its sponsored committee.

CONCLUSION: If all of these conditions are met, CAUSE will not qualify as a committee by virtue of forgiving the $13,000 loan to its sponsored committee: (1) The sponsor does not make or receive a sufficient amount of contributions or independent expenditures, other than those in support of its sponsored committee, to satisfy the thresholds set forth in Government Code Section 82013; (2) The sponsored committee reports all contributions and expenditures made in support of the committee by the sponsor its intermediate units, and the members of such entities; (3) The sponsored committee reports as an intermediary the sponsor; and (4) A responsible officer of the sponsor, as well as the treasurer of the sponsored committee, verifies the committee's campaign statement pursuant to Government Code Section 81004.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission March 21, 199’ Sam McCall Chief Legal Counsel California Union of Safety Employees 2029 H Street Sacramento, CA 95814 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-95-085 Dear Mr. McCall: This letter confirms the advice I provided to you on behalf of the California Union of Safety Employees (CAUSE) regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”) 1 In our telephone conversation of March 6, 1995, you asked whjther CAUSE would have reporting obligations if CAUSE forgave a $13,000 loan that CAUSE made to its sponsored committee. As we discussed in our telephone conversation, the forgiveness of the $13,000 loan could be considered an contribution which could qualify CAUSE as a committee pursuant to Section 82013(c). However, Regulation 18419(c) provides that the sponsor will not qualify as a committee so long as: (1) The sponsor does not make or receive a sufficient amount of contributions or independent expenditures, other than those in support of its sponsored committee, to satisfy the thresholds set forth in Government Code Section 82013. (2) The sponsored committee reports all contributions and expenditures made in support of the committee by the sponsor its intermediate units, and the members of such entities. 1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections 18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Our File No. A-95—085 Page 2 (3) The sponsored committee reports as an intermediary the sponsor. . . ; and (4) A responsible officer of the sponsor, as well as the treasurer of the sponsored contmittee,erifies the conuuittee’s campaign statement pursuant to Government Code Section 81004. If all of these conditions are met, CAUSE will not qualify as a committee by virtue of forgiving the $13,000 loan to its sponsored committee. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322_590l.2 Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell a1 Counsel 2 Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a cost of 10 per page. Wallace 1, Legal Division
embedding.summary: The FPPC advised that loan forgiveness by CAUSE to its sponsored committee could constitute a contribution, but CAUSE would not qualify as a committee if specific reporting and verification conditions are satisfied.
